title: Mesembryanthemum zeyheri
taxonomy: Mesembryanthemum zeyheri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It belongs to the genus Mesembryanthemum, which consists of about 100 species of succulent plants. The species is native to South Africa and is also known as the "blistered iceplant". Mesembryanthemum zeyheri is further classified as a succulent, a type of plant that stores water in its leaves or stems. This classification helps to identify and distinguish it from other species within the Aizoaceae family.
uses: Mesembryanthemum zeyheri, also known as the Zeyher's ice plant, has been found to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits. Further studies are required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.

preparations: Mesembryanthemum zeyheri, also known as the "glass datallow", is used in traditional medicine. intro: Mesembryanthemum zeyheri, commonly known as the succulent plant or ice plant, is a low-growing, spreading succulent native to South Africa. The health benefits of Mesembryanthemum zeyheri include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mesembryanthemum zeyheri include topical creams, ointments, and gels for wound healing, as well as oral supplements in the form of capsules or teas for general health promotion.
